title: Shader Alchemy Lab: Dissolve & Refraction
description: The user operates a shader alchemy lab where a WaterBottle sits atop the InterpolationTest grid used as a mystical pedestal. Through a series of interactions, the user applies custom shader effects—dissolve, chromatic refraction, and animated noise—to both objects, blending them into a unified magical artifact.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements an interactive shader alchemy lab. Load both GLB assets using GLTFLoader: WaterBottle (from shared_assets/WaterBottle.glb) and InterpolationTest (from shared_assets/InterpolationTest.glb). Position the InterpolationTest model as a flat pedestal at y=0, scaled to roughly 2x2 units. Place the WaterBottle centered on top of it at approximately y=0.5, scaled so it sits naturally on the pedestal. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 2, 4) looking at the origin, with OrbitControls enabled. Add ambient light (intensity 0.4) and a directional light from (3, 5, 2) with intensity 1.0. Implement these 5 interaction steps: Step 1: A button labeled 'Apply Dissolve' applies a custom ShaderMaterial to the WaterBottle that uses a noise-based dissolve effect controlled by a uniform threshold (0 to 1). The dissolve edge should glow orange. Step 2: A slider labeled 'Dissolve Amount' lets the user scrub the dissolve threshold from 0.0 to 1.0 in real time, revealing or hiding parts of the bottle. Step 3: A button labeled 'Refraction Mode' replaces the pedestal (InterpolationTest) material with a custom ShaderMaterial that samples a cubemap-style fake refraction using screen-space UV distortion based on view direction and a chromatic aberration offset, giving it a glassy rainbow look. Step 4: A button labeled 'Link Artifacts' activates an animated shader uniform that pulses a glowing energy ring (using sin-based emissive bands in the fragment shader) on both objects simultaneously, synced by shared elapsed time. Step 5: A 'Reset All' button restores original PBR materials on both models. Use dat.gui or simple HTML overlay buttons/sliders. All custom shaders should be written inline as vertex/fragment shader strings. The dissolve shader must use a 3D simplex or value noise function. Ensure the render loop updates all shader uniforms each frame with a clock delta.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  dissolveThreshold: number,         // current dissolve threshold value for WaterBottle shader (0.0 to 1.0)
  isDissolveApplied: boolean,         // whether dissolve shader has been applied to WaterBottle
  isRefractionApplied: boolean,         // whether refraction shader has been applied to pedestal
  pedestalRotationSpeed: number,         // rotation speed of InterpolationTest pedestal in radians per frame
  bottleVerticalOffset: number,         // vertical position offset of WaterBottle from pedestal surface
  glowIntensity: number,         // intensity of dissolve edge glow effect (0.0 to 5.0)
  noiseFrequency: number,         // frequency multiplier for noise pattern in shaders
  chromaticAberration: number,         // chromatic aberration strength in refraction shader (0.0 to 0.1)
  animationTime: number,         // elapsed time in seconds for shader animation
  cameraDistance: number,         // distance of camera from origin point
  activeShaderCount: number,         // count of active custom shaders currently applied (0 to 2)
}
